functional_state.detail: Clean first-run SaneScan install with no saved documents. Launch with --sanescan-reset-library.

output_assertions: Scan opens or requests the native document-scanner flow.
Import opens the native Photos picker.

workflow.outcome: Empty library scan and import actions are reachable passed with Mini source/test/screenshot evidence
steps_completed: Launch a clean install with the reset-library argument.
Verify the empty library shows Scan and Import as the primary customer actions.
Open the Scan action and verify the system document-scanner path is requested when camera hardware is available.
Open the Import action and verify the Photos picker appears.

functional_state.detail: Seeded local fixture library with contract, receipt, and clinic form documents. Launch with --sanescan-reset-library and --sanescan-ui-fixtures.

output_assertions: Document detail opens with OCR text.
Export opens the native share sheet.

workflow.outcome: Fixture documents open, OCR is visible, and PDF export reaches the share sheet passed with Mini source/test/screenshot evidence
steps_completed: Verify the scanned page image, recognized text, and export action are visible.
Tap Export and verify the native share sheet appears.

functional_state.detail: Fixture library with paywall preview products enabled. Launch with --sanescan-reset-library, --sanescan-ui-fixtures, and --sanescan-paywall-preview.

output_assertions: Paywall displays subscription details, legal links, restore purchases, and purchase option copy.

workflow.outcome: Paywall subscription purchase flow includes legal links and restore passed with Mini source/test/screenshot evidence
steps_completed: Launch with fixture documents and paywall preview enabled.
Verify product option copy, subscription details, Terms of Use, Privacy Policy, Restore Purchases, and Done are visible.
Verify Terms and Privacy are functional links.

functional_state.detail: Current release screenshot bundle in docs/images plus visual-audit receipts. Use the standard visual audit screenshot runner on the Mini.

inputs: Review every customer-facing App Store screenshot.
output_assertions: Screenshot bundle is visually balanced, clear, dark-mode only, and accurately represents the app.

workflow.outcome: App Store screenshots represent customer-facing iPhone and iPad surfaces passed with Mini source/test/screenshot evidence
steps_completed: Generate iPhone and iPad dark-mode screenshot sets from the same customer-facing app states.
Inspect every screenshot for stretched documents, distorted logos, status-bar artifacts, clipping, overlap, and unreadable text.
Upload the approved screenshot set to App Store Connect.

evidence.detail: Tests/CustomerUIActions.yml contains "iPad screenshots are native iPad compositions"

evidence.limitation: This sweep verifies customer-visible safe surfaces with current source/test guards, approved screenshots, local StoreKit configuration, and UI-test evidence. Real-device VisionKit proof still requires attached iPhone hardware.

product_quality_review.note: Professional product-quality checklist covering UI/UX, screenshots, marketing parity, App Store readiness, monetization, accessibility, recovery, performance, and funnel telemetry.

items.question: Does the first screen make it obvious that SaneScan scans documents into private searchable PDFs?
evidence: Current release-required customer UI action evidence covers this question.
items.notes: Covered by current SaneScan source guards, screenshots, metadata, or UI-test evidence.

items.question: Is the promised job concrete enough that a new user knows when to use SaneScan instead of Files, Notes, or Adobe Scan?
evidence: Current release-required customer UI action evidence covers this question.
items.notes: Covered by current SaneScan source guards, screenshots, metadata, or UI-test evidence.

items.question: Can a first-time user start scanning or importing without reading instructions or entering unnecessary data?
evidence: Current release-required customer UI action evidence covers this question.
items.notes: Covered by current SaneScan source guards, screenshots, metadata, or UI-test evidence.

items.question: Does the empty state show primary actions in a clear visual hierarchy with no dead or disabled-looking controls?

items.question: Does tapping Scan open the real VisionKit scanner or clearly explain the hardware/simulator limitation?
evidence: Simulator/source proof exists, but real-device VisionKit camera proof is still pending.
items.notes: Needs iPhone hardware proof because VisionKit scanner availability differs from simulator.

items.question: Does tapping Import open the native Photos picker without asking for broader permissions than needed?

items.question: Is the public CTA aligned with the actual live App Store state and not still saying coming soon after READY_FOR_SALE?

items.question: Does the paywall show the real subscription period, price, renewal behavior, cancellation path, and value proposition before purchase?

items.question: Are Terms, Privacy, and Restore Purchases visible and functional inside the paywall flow?

items.question: Can a sandbox purchase or purchase-failure path be completed without trapping the user in loading or unavailable state?
evidence: Annual-only StoreKit configuration and paywall copy are verified, but live sandbox purchase, cancellation/failure, and restore proof is not available in this headless Mini run.
items.notes: Needs Xcode StoreKit environment, sandbox/TestFlight purchase, or attached device proof for product load, purchase success/failure/cancel, and restore.

items.question: Are primary controls labeled for VoiceOver and reachable in a sensible order?
evidence: UI tests assert named controls on library, detail, and paywall surfaces and dump accessibility hierarchies for review.

items.question: Does the interface remain usable with larger Dynamic Type or accessibility text sizes?

items.question: Is contrast high enough in dark mode, with no low-opacity gray text on dark backgrounds?
